summary: Fantastic soundboard capture of a high‑energy 1979 show with standout Dancin’ in the Streets, Scarlet > Fire and a sweet Playin’.
review: The recording is a crystal‑clear soundboard mix that lets every nuance of the band shine, while the performance itself is classic late‑'79 energy – a hopping first set highlighted by an extended “Dancin’ in the Streets,” and a second set that builds into a gorgeous Scarlet > Fire transition, a soaring Playin’, and a tight spacey outro jam.  Even the occasional vocal wobble on Terrapin Station can’t dull the overall brilliance of this show.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers gave 5‑star ratings, praising both the musical performance and the recording quality; the consensus is uniformly enthusiastic.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Fantastic clarity and balance throughout; minimal deviation from optimal sound, no noticeable drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set bounces with high‑energy hops and a marathon Dancin', while the second set builds a pre‑drums run that culminates in a spacey yet tight outro jam, delivering classic 1979 momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for superb guitar work in Scarlet/Fire transition and an outstanding Playin'; vocal hiccups noted on Terrapin Station.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drums run and outro jam described as tight, spacey and a true work of art.
